label: Stage 124
scope: Decide whether to open a compact systematic short n-tuple additive pilot and document why several previously excluded optimization lanes remain non-reopen recommendations under the current browser-target runtime.

why: Stage 52 explicitly said the evaluator lane should reopen only when the layout family changes, not for another retraining pass of the same family.
Stage 69 follow-up kept the active baseline because same-family micro-patches improved local fidelity but did not beat the baseline on the fresh noisy match set.

The current runtime already has built-in compact systematic adjacent-pair layout families and end-to-end training/export/benchmark tooling, so a bounded pilot is operationally cheap compared with adding a new search subsystem.
The active tuple residual is still small (24 size-2 tuples, 216 table cells, late-b/endgame only), leaving room for a compact additive follow-up without replacing the existing evaluator lane.

recommendedScope.mainPilot: diagonal-adjacent-pairs-full-v1 as a genuinely new short systematic family
recommendedScope.controlPilot: orthogonal-adjacent-pairs-outer2-v1 or orthogonal-adjacent-pairs-full-v1 as a same-direction control
recommendedScope.avoidFirst: straight-adjacent-pairs-full-v1 due to its much larger compact module cost
acceptanceCriteria: exact/depth parity remains safe on the formal validation set
holdout search fidelity improves over the active baseline
fresh noisy match benchmarks do not fall behind baseline
module size and startup cost stay within a browser-friendly budget

extraMctsLateLaneRetuning.reason: Stage 118 closed the remaining PN/PPN retuning candidates and Stage 119 showed the last meaningful hot-path refactor attempt was slower.
extraMctsLateLaneRetuning.hiddenSubcandidateCheck: Only reopen for a genuinely new architectural idea or a new failing late-lane benchmark corpus; do not reopen for more threshold/bias/gate retuning.

moveOrderingRetuning.reason: Stage 45 explicitly froze move-ordering at candidateH2 and switched priority to MPC/runtime plumbing after gains plateaued.
moveOrderingRetuning.hiddenSubcandidateCheck: Re-run compatibility replay only after a new evaluator/tuple family actually changes the search landscape.

traditionalHandcraftedEvaluatorExpansion.reason: Stage 13 found only small remaining issues and directed the repo toward data-driven tuning rather than adding more hand-written heuristics.
traditionalHandcraftedEvaluatorExpansion.hiddenSubcandidateCheck: Only tiny cleanup or dead-calculation removal is still plausible; no broad new manual feature family looks essential.

fiveToSixEmptiesMicroSpecialization.reason: Stages 23, 24, and 84 already specialized the exact tail and settled on threshold 6; threshold 8 did not generalize as a default and current profiling no longer points here as the primary bottleneck.
fiveToSixEmptiesMicroSpecialization.hiddenSubcandidateCheck: Reopen only if future profiling after other hot-path changes shows the 5–6 empties family becoming the dominant hotspot again.

specialEndingExpansion.reason: Stage 98 productized the shared special-ending safety net and regression suite for both classic and MCTS lanes.
specialEndingExpansion.hiddenSubcandidateCheck: Treat new trap sequences as regression additions first; do not widen the lane without a new recurring failure class.

hiddenMustHaveAudit.conclusion: No open TODO/FIXME/HACK backlog was found in js/ai or js/core, so the remaining must-have candidate is not hidden in source comments.

recommendedNextStep.summary: Proceed only with a bounded compact systematic short n-tuple additive pilot built on a new layout family, then rerun move-ordering compatibility and noisy match checks before any runtime adoption decision.
notRecommendedNow: additional move-ordering retuning before evaluator changes
new 5–6 empties micro-specialization before profiling shifts
broad special-ending expansion without a new trap corpus
